warnings:
git clone failed: Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/ufl/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/FEniCS/ufl/': Error in the HTTP2 framing layer
recommendations: add a minimal CI smoke test for the generated MCP plugin (import plugin
start server
call 2–3 core endpoints)
populate `test status`/`code review`/`performance metrics` in the pipeline output and fail on missing sections to avoid blind spots
fix dependency detection so `pyproject.toml` is recognized (it currently reports `pyproject: False` despite file presence)
deduplicate/normalize exported endpoints in MCP generation (e.g.
repeated `expr`/`form`) and enforce naming conventions
add contract tests that compare MCP endpoint behavior to direct `ufl` API results for key symbolic ops (`grad`
`div`
`derivative`
`replace`)
introduce type-validation/error-shape tests for MCP inputs using Pydantic schemas
create a small benchmark suite for hot paths (`apply_derivatives`
`compute_form_data`
form hashing/signature) with regression thresholds in CI
split very large algorithm modules (notably `ufl/algorithms/apply_derivatives.py`) into focused submodules to reduce maintenance risk
add property-based tests (Hypothesis) for expression DAG transformations and algebraic invariants
strengthen docs by mapping each exposed MCP endpoint to its UFL module/class and including canonical request/response examples
add compatibility matrix testing across supported Python versions and NumPy ranges (plus optional SymPy-present environment)
improve release hygiene with automated changelog checks and wheel/sdist verification before publish
add observability in MCP service (structured logs
latency/error counters per endpoint)
include security/dependency scanning (pip-audit/Safety + GitHub Dependabot policy enforcement) in CI
add golden tests for signature/stability-sensitive outputs to catch subtle symbolic canonicalization regressions

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status `success` and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Robust fallback behavior: after git clone failure, pipeline continued via zip/import fallback and still analyzed ~217 files.
Import-mode adapter generation succeeded and MCP service health check passed (`healthy`, stdio transport).
Low intrusiveness risk and high import feasibility (0.95) supported non-invasive wrapping strategy.
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed in DownloadNode due to network/protocol error (`HTTP2 framing layer`) when cloning from GitHub.
Pipeline observability gaps: zeroed/empty metrics (`llm_calls`, `total_files_processed`, performance metrics, code review) reduce confidence in reported success quality.
Dependency detection inconsistency: `pyproject.toml` exists but reported as `pyproject: False`, indicating analyzer defect.
Test signal quality is weak: original project tests marked not passed despite `100%` coverage and zero execution time, indicating reporting inconsistency.

node_performance.download_time: Download encountered hard failure in git clone; recovery occurred through fallback path. Time likely dominated by retry/failure latency.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis appears effective despite fallback (file inventory, core module extraction, risk/security summaries), but metadata counters are inconsistent.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed quickly enough within total 241s and produced expected scaffold files; however endpoint/tool count reporting is contradictory.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed, but project test execution data is effectively missing (0s runtime, empty details), limiting reliability.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB). Instrumentation required before concluding efficiency.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0%). No actionable CPU profile available.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate; repository analyzed and plugin scaffold generated, but generated LOC/size reported as 0 suggests accounting bug.
